headline: AI moves from chatbot to coworker mode—orgs racing to adapt before the paradigm shift hits harder

stories.title: McKinsey's AI Trust Maturity Survey Shows Gap Between Execution and Strategy
stories.summary: Orgs are scaling agentic AI deployment but barely one-third have solid governance. McKinsey found RAI maturity jumped to 2.3 in 2026, but leadership and oversight are lagging behind tech rollout.
stories.takeaway: The real bottleneck isn't capability—it's getting the org structure right fast enough to avoid chaos when AI actually starts running core workflows autonomously.

stories.title: OpenAI's Competitive Moat Keeps Shrinking—Here's Why Nobody Has a Plan
stories.summary: Benedict Evans breaks down why OpenAI's user base, tech, and stickiness don't add up to durable advantage. The incumbents matched the models, and new experiences will decide winners.
stories.takeaway: OpenAI's hunting for strategic positions before market maturity hits, but unlike Google/Apple in their eras, they don't have that *one thing* that truly works and no one else can replicate.

stories.title: AI Absorption Crisis: Capabilities Growing Way Faster Than Organizations Can Deploy
stories.summary: Ethan Mollick on the exponential curve—multimodal, video, reasoning are improving stupidly fast, but businesses are stuck in 'rolling disruption' cycles. Each new capability reshape hits before the last one lands.
stories.takeaway: Companies need to stop thinking of AI as a one-time transformation and start treating it as continuous waves of change that'll reshape workflows every few months, not years.

stories.title: AI-Powered Advertising Exploding: 63% Growth to $57B in 2026
stories.summary: Marketing automation is no longer optional—AI-driven ad platforms now dominate targeting and optimization. Brands trusting algorithms over manual control as performance wins.
stories.takeaway: Marketing teams gotta accept that AI optimization is the new baseline. The era of hands-on campaign tweaking is basically dead for anyone at scale.

watchNext.title: EU AI Act Enforcement Hits Full Throttle in 2026—Global Ripples Coming
watchNext.why: Most consequential tech regulation since GDPR just entered enforcement phase. If you're building or deploying AI anywhere, this matters. Everyone's scrambling to comply.

watchNext.title: a16z's Big Ideas 2026: AI Eating the Real World (Not Just Software)
watchNext.why: Andreessen's saying the next decade belongs to companies building AI-native infrastructure—robotics, energy systems, autonomous sensors. First software ate the world; now software eats factories.
